originalTitle: Australia news live: trade minister demands removal of ‘unjustified’ US tariffs; deadly bird flu detected in Queensland for the first time
content: Ultra-long distance Qantas test flight touches down in Melbourne after 19-hour journey The world’s longest-range commercial aircraft has been greeted by plane-spotters and aviation enthusiasts after arriving in Melbourne on Friday morning. The Airbus A350-1000ULR, built for Qantas to fly about 20 hours non-stop between Sydney and London, completed a 19-hour odyssey from Toulouse, France that became one of the most-watched flights in the world. More than 74,000 people tracked its path on flighradar24.com as it prepared to land. 11-year-old Will took the day off school to watch its descent, appearing with a crowd of other enthusiasts on Sunrise. Labor avoids fight on gambling reform An issue that was shaping up to be one of the more contentious policy battlegrounds has also avoided a fight on the floor - with some amendments to be agreed to today. Gambling has been a hot button issue both inside and outside the party, and teams were working overnight to figure out how to get stronger commitments into the party platform. Instead of a fight, the minister is understood to have agreed to the wording of the amendments (which we’ll bring you as soon as we get them). Aukus construction yards to be ‘pride of the nation’ The defence minister, Richard Marles, is speaking in Adelaide after announcing an extra $4.6 billion for construction yards that will build Australia’s nuclear-powered submarine fleet. In the coming years as we get to a number of north of 10,000 people, and they are jobs that will be here for generations to come. This is a hugely significant facility in terms of what our nation’s industrial capacity is. This will be the highest tech production line in Australia, it will be one of the highest tech production lines in the world. I really want to thank the more than a thousand workers now, but thousands to come who will be building this facility. They should be incredibly proud of what they are doing, and the outcome of this will really be the pride of the nation. Aukus is expected to be discussed at Labor’s national conference on the weekend, following calls to review the plan. Earlier, Marles said the issue had already been debated at the previous national conference, where iit received “overwhelming support”. Farrell silent on donations from Chinese billionaire Don Farrell, the trade minister and special minister of state, has brushed off questions about Anthony Albanese attending a fundraising dinner hosted by the son of a Chinese billionaire under scrutiny from the tax office. The Australian Financial Review reported this week Albanese attended the event with 25-year-old Felix Lee, whose father, Phillip Dong Fang Lee, has made big donations to the Labor party. The Sydney Morning Herald reported on Friday that Phillip Lee has been investigated by the ATO over large sums of money coming from China to Australia. Other attenders at the fundraiser included the Commonwealth Bank boss, Matt Comyn, former Victorian premier Daniel Andrews, the former Asic chairman James Shipton and former Future Fund boss David Murray. Asked if the fundraising was appropriate, Farrell said he was “unfamiliar” with the media reports. All of the issues in relation to donations are matters that are helped with and dealt with by the party administration. Australia’s largest exports to US exempt from latest tariffs Australian gold, beef and copper will continue flowing into the US tariff-free, despite Donald Trump’s latest wave of levies. The trade minister, Don Farrell, said those exports would continue to have a high volume of trade into the US. The 67% increase in trade has been because those products – the value of those products, and the volume of those products – has been increasing. I don’t see any reason to think that that’s not going to continue. I think we will continue to have a high volume of trade into the United States. But we’re always looking for new markets. Tariffs on Australian exports ‘completely unjustified’ The trade minister, Don Farrell, is speaking in Adelaide after the US revealed it would slap 12.5% tariffs on Australian goods in a trade move hitting dozens of countries that Donald Trump claims have not done enough to combat forced labour in supply chains. Farrell said: We do not believe it was justified. We believe that – amongst all of the countries in the world -Australia does take the issue of modern slavery seriously, and will continue to do that. The Albanese government believes that this imposition of higher tariffs on Australia is completely unjustified, and we will continue to lobby the United States trade representative to remove all tariffs on Australian goods. Relationship between police and Jewish security to be probed as royal commission continues The relationship between police and Jewish security groups is set to be examined after calls for governments to entirely fund the community’s security needs. Across a fifth round of hearings in Melbourne this week, the anti-Semitism royal commission has been told the financial burden of Jewish safety in Australia is largely being worn by families, schools, places of worship and community groups, AAP reports. Liberal MP Julian Leeser, whose persistent lobbying was instrumental to establishing the landmark inquiry, will appear before the commission on Friday to detail the rising tide of hate speech and its impact on the community. We’ll be bringing you the latest on the blog. Australia’s hidden pain forces many out of workforce Millions of Australians living with chronic pain are fighting an invisible battle that extends far beyond health care. It’s a condition that forces many to leave the workforce, with fresh research revealing that 42% of sufferers have had to stop working due to chronic pain, AAP reports. The 2026 National Pain Report also revealed that 57% of respondents reported their conditions had affected their ability to work. Advocate Kellie Jade Ellis told AAP: I felt like I couldn’t disclose my chronic pain to any
chineseTitle: 澳大利亚新闻直播：贸易部长要求取消“不合理”的美国关税；昆士兰州首次发现致命禽流感
chineseContent: 澳洲航空超长距离试飞经过 19 小时的飞行后抵达墨尔本 这架世界上航程最长的商用飞机于周五早上抵达墨尔本后，受到了飞机爱好者和航空爱好者的欢迎。这架为澳洲航空制造的空客 A350-1000ULR 飞机可在悉尼和伦敦之间直飞约 20 小时，完成了从法国图卢兹起飞的 19 小时的长途飞行，成为世界上最受关注的航班之一。当它准备着陆时，超过 74,000 人在 flighradar24.com 上追踪了它的路径。 11 岁的威尔请了一天假去观看它的下降，与一群其他爱好者一起出现在日出号上。工党避免在赌博改革问题上展开斗争 一个即将成为更具争议性的政策战场之一的问题也避免了一场现场斗争——今天将就一些修正案达成一致。赌博一直是党内外的一个热点问题，各团队连夜努力找出如何让党纲得到更强有力的承诺。据了解，部长没有发生争执，而是同意了修正案的措辞（我们一收到修正案就会立即给您带来）。奥库斯建造厂将成为“国家的骄傲” 国防部长理查德·马尔斯 (Richard Marles) 在阿德莱德发表讲话，此前他宣布额外拨款 46 亿澳元用于建造厂，用于建造澳大利亚的核动力潜艇舰队。在未来的几年里，当我们的人口数量超过 10,000 人时，他们的工作岗位将会为子孙后代留下。就我国的工业能力而言，这是一个非常重要的设施。这将是澳大利亚技术含量最高的生产线，也将是世界上技术含量最高的生产线之一。我真的要感谢超过
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
